| 产品定位 | NVIDIA NVL72 is a rack-scale high-bandwidth domain built around the Blackwell generation. It uses 36 GB200 superchips to form a 72-GPU NVLink domain and represents the current industrial benchmark for vertically integrated SuperPod engineering.

### 适用负载

- dense training
- TP-heavy workloads
- large-model inference

### 雷达图摘要

> NVL72 sits near the Pareto frontier on latency, memory semantics and ecosystem maturity. Its main trade-offs are fixed topology and very high power/cooling requirements, rather than software readiness.


### 委员会评语

> Example committee assessment based on public materials only. NVL72 is a canonical benchmark product for SPI because it combines advanced packaging, native rack-scale interconnect and mature software into one deliverable system. It should be interpreted as a high-maturity reference point rather than as a vendor-verified SPI submission.
